title: picea engelmannii var. columbiana
intro: Picea engelmannii var. columbiana, also known as the Engelmann spruce, is a variant of the Engelmann spruce tree species native to the western parts of North America. The essential oils extracted from its needles have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Picea engelmannii var. columbiana include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of essential oils, teas, and infused products, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
